author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the words of the Gospel, John vi. 53, ‘Except ye eat the flesh,’ etc., and on the words of the apostles. And the Psalms. Against the Pelagians.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Delivered at the Table of the Martyr St. Cyprian, the 9th

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: of the Calends of October,—23 Sept., on the Lord’s day.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We have heard the True Master, the Divine Redeemer, the human Saviour, commending to us our Ransom, His Blood.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He spake to us of His Body and Blood; He called His Body Meat, His Blood Drink.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The faithful recognise the Sacrament of the faithful.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But the hearers what else do they but hear?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When therefore commending such Meat and such Drink He said, “Except ye shall eat My Flesh and drink My Blood, ye shall have no life in you;” (and this that He said concerning life, who else said it but the Life Itself?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But that man shall have death, not life, who shall think that the Life is false), His disciples were offended, not all of them indeed, but very many, saying within themselves, “This is an hard saying, who can hear it?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But when the Lord knew this in Himself, and heard the murmurings of their thought, He answered them, thinking though uttering nothing, that they might understand that they were heard, and might cease to entertain such thoughts.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then did He answer?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Doth this offend you?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “What then if ye shall see the Son of Man ascend up where He was before?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What meaneth this?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Doth this offend you?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Do ye imagine that I am about to make divisions of this My Body which ye see; and to cut up My Members, and give them to you?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: ‘What then if ye shall see the Son of Man ascend up where He was before?’”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Assuredly, He who could ascend Whole could not be consumed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then He both gave us of His Body and Blood a healthful refreshment, and briefly solved so great a question as to His Own Entireness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let them then who eat, eat on, and them that drink, drink; let them hunger and thirst; eat Life, drink Life.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That eating, is to be refreshed; but thou art in such wise refreshed, as that that whereby thou art refreshed, faileth not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That drinking, what is it but to live?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Eat Life, drink Life; thou shalt have life, and the Life is Entire.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But then this shall be, that is, the Body and the Blood of Christ shall be each man’s Life; if what is taken in the Sacrament visibly is in the truth itself eaten spiritually, drunk spiritually.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For we have heard the Lord Himself saying, “It is the Spirit That quickeneth, but the flesh profiteth nothing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The words that I have spoken unto you, are Spirit and Life.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But there are some of you,” saith He, “that believe not.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Such were they who said, “This is a hard saying, who can hear it?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is hard, but only to the hard; that is, it is incredible, but only to the incredulous.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But in order to teach us that this very believing is matter of gift, not of desert, He saith, “As I have said unto you, no man cometh unto Me, except it were given him of My Father.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now as to where the Lord said this, if we call to mind the foregoing words of the Gospel, we shall find that He had said, “No man cometh unto Me, except the Father which hath sent Me draw him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He did not lead, but draw.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This violence is done to the heart, not the body.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why then dost thou marvel?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Believe, and thou comest; love, and thou art drawn.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do not suppose here any rough and uneasy violence; it is gentle, it is sweet; it is the very sweetness that draweth thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Is not a sheep drawn, when fresh grass is shown to it in its hunger?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet I imagine that it is not bodily driven on, but fast bound by desire.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In such wise do thou come too to Christ; do not conceive of long journeyings; where thou believest, there thou comest.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For unto Him, who is everywhere we come by love, not by sailing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But forasmuch as even in this kind of voyage, waves and tempests of divers temptations abound; believe on the Crucified; that thy faith may be able to ascend the Wood.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou shalt not sink, but shalt be borne upon the Wood.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thus, even thus, amid the waves of this world did he sail, who said, “But God forbid that I should glory, save in the Cross of our Lord Jesus Christ.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But wonderful it is, that when Christ Crucified is preached, two hear, one despiseth, the other ascendeth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let him that despiseth, impute it to himself; let not him that ascendeth, arrogate it to himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For he hath heard from the True Master; “No man cometh unto Me, except it were given unto him of My Father.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let him joy, that it hath been given; let him render thanks to Him who giveth it, with a humble, not an arrogant heart; lest what he hath attained through humility, he lose through pride.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For even they who are already walking in this way of righteousness, if they attribute it to themselves, and to their own strength, perish out of it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And therefore Holy Scripture teaching us humility saith by the Apostle, “Work out your own salvation with fear and trembling.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And lest hereupon they should attribute ought to themselves, because he said, “Work,” he subjoined immediately, “For it is God who worketh in you both to will and to do of His good pleasure.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “It is God who worketh in you;” therefore “with fear and trembling,” make a valley, receive the rain.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Low grounds are filled, high grounds are dried up.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Grace is rain.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why dost thou marvel then, if “God resist the proud, and giveth grace unto the lowly”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore, “with fear and trembling;” that is, with humility.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Be not high-minded, but fear.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Fear that thou mayest be filled; be not high-minded, lest thou be dried up.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But you will say, “I am walking in this way already; once there was need for me to learn, there was need for me to know by the teaching of the law what I had to do: now I have the free choice of the will; who shall withdraw me from this way?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou read carefully, thou wilt find that a certain man began to uplift himself, on a certain abundance of his, which he had nevertheless received; but that the Lord in mercy, to teach him humility, took away what He had given; and he was on a sudden reduced to poverty, and confessing the mercy of God in his recollection, he said, “In my abundance I said, I shall never be moved.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “In my abundance I said.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But I said it, I who am a man said it; “All men are liars, I said.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore, “in my abundance I said;” so great was the abundance, that I dared to say, “I shall never be moved.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “O Lord, in Thy favour Thou gavest strength to my beauty.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But “Thou turnedst away Thy Face from me, and I was troubled.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Thou hast shown me,” saith he, “that that wherein I did abound, was of Thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast shown me Whence I should seek, to Whom attribute what I had received, to Whom I ought to render thanks, to Whom I should run in my thirst, Whereby be filled, and with Whom keep that whereby I should be filled.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: ‘For my strength will I keep to Thee;’ whereby I am by Thy bounty filled, through Thy safe keeping I will not lose.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: ‘My strength will I keep to Thee.’

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That Thou mightest show me this, ‘Thou turnedst away Thy Face from me, and I was troubled.’

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: ‘Troubled,’ because dried up; dried up, because exalted.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Say then thou dry and parched one, that thou mayest be filled again; ‘My soul is as earth without water unto Thee.’

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Say, ‘My soul is as earth without water unto Thee.’

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For Thou hast said, not the Lord, ‘I shall never be moved.’

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast said it, presuming on thine own strength; but it was not of thyself, and thou didst think as if it were.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then doth the Lord say?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Serve ye the Lord in fear, and rejoice unto Him with trembling.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So the Apostle too, “Work out your own salvation with fear and trembling.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For it is God who worketh in you.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore rejoice with trembling: “Lest at any time the Lord be angry.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I see that you anticipate me by your crying out.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For you know what I am about to say, you anticipate it by crying out.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And whence have ye this, but that He taught you to whom ye have by believing come?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This then He saith; hear what ye know already; I am not teaching, but in preaching am calling to your remembrance; nay, I am neither teaching, seeing that ye know already, nor calling to remembrance, seeing that ye remember, but let us say all together what together with us ye retain.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Embrace discipline, and rejoice,” but, “with trembling,” that, humble ye may ever hold fast that which ye have received.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Lest at any time the Lord be angry;” with the proud of course, attributing to themselves what they have, not rendering thanks to Him, from whom they have.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Lest at any time the Lord be angry, and ye perish from the righteous way.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Did he say, Lest at any time the Lord be angry, and ye come not into the righteous way”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Did he say, “Lest the Lord be angry, and He bring you not to the righteous way”? or “admit you not into the righteous way?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye are walking in it already, be not proud, lest ye even perish from it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: ‘And ye perish,’ saith he, ‘from the righteous way.’”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “When His wrath shall be kindled in a short time” against you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: At no distant time.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As soon as thou art proud, thou losest at once what thou hadst received.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As though man terrified by all this were to say, “What shall I do then?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It follows, “Blessed are all they that trust in Him:” not in themselves, but in Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “By grace are we saved, not of ourselves, but it is the gift of God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Peradventure ye are saying, “What does he mean, that he is so often saying this?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A second and a third time he says it; and scarcely ever speaks, but when he says it.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Would that I may not say it in vain!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For men there are unthankful to grace, attributing much to poor and disabled nature.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: True it is, when man was created he received great power of free-will; but he lost it by sin.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He fell into death, became infirm, was left in the way by the robbers half dead; the Samaritan, which is by interpretation keeper, passing by lifted him up on his own beast; he is still being brought to the inn.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why is he lifted up?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He is still in process of curing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “But,” he will say, “it is enough for me that in baptism I received remission of all sins.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because iniquity was blotted out, was therefore infirmity brought to an end?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I received,” says he, “remission of all sins.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is quite true.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: All sins were blotted out in the Sacrament of Baptism, all entirely, of words, deeds, thoughts, all were blotted out.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But this is the “oil and wine” which was poured in by the way.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye remember, beloved Brethren, that man who was wounded by the robbers, and half dead by the way, how he was strengthened, by receiving oil and wine for his wounds.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: His error indeed was already pardoned, and yet his weakness is in process of healing in the inn.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The inn, if ye recognise it, is the Church.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In the time present, an inn, because in life we are passing by: it will be a home, whence we shall never remove, when we shall have got in perfect health unto the kingdom of heaven.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Meanwhile receive we gladly our treatment in the inn, and weak as we still are, glory we not of sound health: lest through our pride we gain nothing else, but never for all our treatment to be cured.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Bless the Lord, O my soul.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Say, yea say to thy soul, “Thou art still in this life, still bearest about a frail flesh, still “doth the corruptible body press down the soul;” still after the entireness of remission hast thou received the remedy of prayer; for still, whilst thy weaknesses are being healed, dost thou say, “Forgive us our debts.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Say then to thy soul, thou lowly valley, not an exalted hill; say to thy soul, “Bless the Lord, O my soul, and forget not all His benefits.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What benefits?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Tell them, enumerate them, render thanks.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What benefits?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Who forgiveth all thine iniquities.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This took place in baptism.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What takes place now?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Who healeth all thy weaknesses.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This takes place now; I acknowledge.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But as long as I am here, “the corruptible body presseth down the soul.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Say then also that which comes next, “Who redeemeth thy life from corruption.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: After redemption from corruption, what remaineth?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “When this corruptible shall have put on incorruption, and this mortal shall have put on immortality, then shall be brought to pass the saying that is written, Death is swallowed up in victory.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Where, O death, is thy contention?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There rightly, “O death, where is thy sting?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou seekest its place, and findest it not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is “the sting of death”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “O death, where is thy sting?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Where is sin?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou seekest, and it is nowhere.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For “the sting of death is sin.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They are the Apostle’s words, not mine.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Then shall it be said, “O death, where is thy sting?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Sin shall nowhere be, neither to surprise thee, nor to assault thee, nor to inflame thy conscience.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Then it shall not be said, “Forgive us our debts.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what shall be said?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “O Lord our God, give us peace: for Thou hast rendered all things unto us.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Finally, after the redemption from all corruption, what remaineth but the crown of righteousness?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This at least remaineth, but even in it, or under it, let not the head be swollen that it may receive the crown.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear, mark well the Psalm, how that crown will not have a swollen head.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: After he had said, “Who redeemeth thy life from corruption;” he saith, “Who crowneth thee.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Here thou wert ready at once to say, “‘Crowneth thee,’ is an acknowledgment of my merits, my own excellence hath done it; it is the payment of a debt, not a gift.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Give ear rather to the Psalm.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For it is thou again that sayest this; and “all men are liars.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear what God saith; “Who crowneth thee with mercy and pity.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Of His mercy He crowneth thee, of His pity He crowneth thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For thou hadst no worthiness that He should call thee, and being called should justify thee, being justified glorify thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The remnant is saved by the election of grace.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if by grace, then is it no more of works; otherwise grace is no more grace.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For to him that worketh, the reward shall not be reckoned according to grace, but according to debt.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Apostle saith, “Not according to grace, but according to debt.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But “thee He crowneth with pity and mercy;” and if thy own merits have gone before, God saith to thee, “Examine well thy merits, and thou shalt see that they are My gifts.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This then is the righteousness of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As it is called, “The Lord’s salvation,” not whereby the Lord is saved, but which He giveth to them whom He saveth; so too the grace of God through Jesus Christ our Lord is called the righteousness of God, not as that whereby the Lord is righteous, but whereby He justifieth those whom of ungodly He maketh righteous.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But some, as the Jews in former times, both wish to be called Christians, and still ignorant of God’s righteousness, desire to establish their own, even in our own times, in the times of open grace, the times of the full revelation of grace which before was hidden; in the times of grace now manifested in the floor, which once lay hid in the fleece.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I see that a few have understood me, that more have not understood, whom I will by no means defraud by keeping silence.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Gideon, one of the righteous men of old, asked for a sign from the Lord, and said, “I pray, Lord, that this fleece which I put in the floor be bedewed,and that the floor be dry.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And it was so; the fleece was bedewed, the whole floor was dry.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In the morning he wrung out the fleece in a basin; forasmuch as to the humble is grace given; and in a basin, ye know what the Lord did to His disciples.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Again, he asked for another sign; “O Lord, I would,” saith he, “that the fleece be dry, the floor bedewed.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And it was so.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Call to mind the time of the Old Testament, grace was hidden in a cloud, as the rain in the fleece.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Mark now the time of the New Testament, consider well the nation of the Jews, thou wilt find it as a dry fleece; whereas the whole world, like that floor, is full of grace, not hidden, but manifested.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wherefore we are forced exceedingly to bewail our brethren, who strive not against hidden, but against open and manifested grace.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There is allowance for the Jews.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What shall we say of Christians?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wherefore are ye enemies to the grace of Christ?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why rely ye on yourselves?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why unthankful?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For why did Christ come?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Was not nature here before?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Was not nature here, which ye only deceive by your excessive praise?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Was not the Law here?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But the Apostle says, “If righteousness come by the Law, then Christ is dead in vain.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What the Apostle says of the Law, that say we of nature to these men.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “If righteousness come by nature, then Christ is dead in vain.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then was said of the Jews, the same altogether do we see in these men now.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “They have a zeal of God: I hear them record that they have a zeal of God, but not according to knowledge.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “not according to knowledge”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For being ignorant of God’s righteousness, and wishing to establish their own, they have not submitted themselves unto the righteousness of God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: My Brethren, share with me in my sorrow.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When ye find such as these, do not hide them; be there no such misdirected mercy in you; by all means, when ye find such, hide them not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Convince the gainsayers, and those who resist, bring to us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For already have two councils on this question been sent to the Apostolic see; and rescripts also have come from thence.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The question has been brought to an issue; would that their error may sometime be brought to an issue too!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore do we advise that they may take heed, we teach that they may be instructed, we pray that they may be changed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us turn to the Lord, etc.
